prose:
Fukui reactivity indices were computed for ferrocene (21 atoms: Fe + two Cp rings) using the GFN2-xTB semi-empirical tight-binding method, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. The N−1 (cation) and N+1 (anion) states used charge ±1 and multiplicity 2 (doublet). Partial charges were obtained via the Mulliken scheme (GFN2-xTB). The geometry was taken as-is from the user-supplied ferrocene.xyz (no re-optimization).

Headline result: The iron atom (atom 11) is both the most electrophilic site (f⁺ = 0.22885456) and most nucleophilic site (f⁻ = 0.25938191) in ferrocene. Its dual descriptor (f⁺ − f⁻ = −0.030527) is negative, indicating net nucleophilic/electron-donor character, consistent with the d⁶ Fe(II) center. All Cp-ring carbons and hydrogens show much smaller, nearly symmetry-equivalent Fukui values. Integrity checks: status = ok, trustworthy = true; sum(f⁺) = sum(f⁻) = 1.0000 (excellent charge conservation). No warnings reported. Engine: xtb (GFN2-xTB) via chemkit. Reference: Bannwarth, C.; Ehlert, S.; Grimme, S. J. Chem. Theory Comput. 2019, 15, 1652–1671. https://doi.org/10.1021/acs.jctc.8b01176
